mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and tightens financial conditions, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days. This is an always-on trend effect independent of any threshold crossing.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS trend and forward 20d returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or periods of rising FEDFUNDS show no significant negative return bias (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes broader economic slowdown, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of UNRATE rising > 0.3pp show positive median 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The real policy rate, approximated by the joint level of FEDFUNDS relative to the trailing CPIAUCSL inflation trend, drives equity valuations: when nominal rates rise faster than inflation (rising real rate), risk premia compress equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. This effect is not decomposable from either series alone.
failure_condition: The combined real-rate proxy (FEDFUNDS minus CPIAUCSL trailing inflation) shows no significant negative correlation with forward 20d returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window, or single-series FEDFUNDS model performs equally well without CPIAUCSL interaction.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in housing starts (HOUST) signal weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand, but this only translates into negative equity returns when the monetary regime is tightening (FEDFUNDS elevated or rising), because in easing regimes housing weakness is offset by expected rate cuts. The mechanism predicts negative 60-day S&P 500 returns conditional on the tightening regime.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines 3 consecutive months while FEDFUNDS > prior 12-month average
failure_condition: During tightening regimes, HOUST declines fail to precede negative 60-day returns in more than 55% of instances, or the effect is equally strong in easing regimes, invalidating the regime-conditionality claim.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained expansion in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy real economic supply-side momentum that supports corporate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week. This is an always-on trend signal with no activation threshold.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO monthly trend and forward 5-day returns stays below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive INDPRO growth periods show no significant excess return versus baseline (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in nonfarm payroll growth (PAYEMS) below a threshold of 100,000 net jobs added per month signals an abrupt weakening in labor demand that triggers short-horizon risk-off equity positioning, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered instances of PAYEMS deceleration below threshold show positive median 5-day forward returns in more than 55% of occurrences, or effect is statistically indistinguishable from random (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales growth, jointly derived from nominal RSAFS growth exceeding CPIAUCSL inflation growth, signals genuine consumer demand strength rather than inflation-driven nominal gains, and predicts positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days. The signal requires both series jointly since nominal RSAFS growth alone can be inflation-driven and non-informative.
failure_condition: Real retail sales proxy (RSAFS growth minus CPIAUCSL growth) shows Rank IC below 0.03 against forward 20d returns over any 2-year window, or nominal RSAFS alone performs equally well without the CPIAUCSL adjustment.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansion phases, positive real GDP growth (GDPC1) confirms sustained aggregate demand strength that supports forward equity valuations over a full quarter, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days. In recessionary or early-recovery regimes this relationship reverses or weakens due to peak-cycle valuation risk.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth quarters during late-cycle expansion fail to produce above-median 60-day forward returns in more than 55% of instances, or the relationship shows equal strength across all cycle phases, invalidating regime-conditionality.
